title: The ONLY 6 Skills You Need to 10x Your Claude Projects
summary: Austin Marchese presents six skills for maximizing Claude project output: web scraping, /ingest-source for knowledge base capture, /improve-system for iterative refinement, /ask-the-board for advisory consultation, /internal-focus-group for testing, and compound engineering for shipping. The workflow is demonstrated through building a fitness trainer project from scratch.
writeup: A walkthrough of six Claude project skills designed to turn a stateless AI session into a compounding, self-improving personal intelligence system. The first two skills build the data foundation: Firecrawl and Exa plugins handle JavaScript-capable scraping and semantic search, then an ingest-source skill pre-analyzes ingested material into a structured knowledge base so Claude does not scan raw text from scratch each session. The remaining skills layer on a feedback loop (a five-mode improve-system skill), two evaluation layers (an expert advisory board cloned from public figures, and a focus-group skill cloning specific real people as audience proxies), and a sixth skill left partially covered by the transcript.
key_points: The web-scraper skill patches two Claude limitations at once: Firecrawl accesses JavaScript-rendered pages that Claude cannot natively load, and Exa replaces keyword search with semantic search that understands intent rather than matching tokens.

The ingest-source skill pre-analyzes ingested PDFs and URLs into a structured index before storing them in the project, so retrieval is a lookup into a table of contents rather than a full-document scan each session.

The improve-system skill runs five modes against the accumulated project state: audit (flag outdated knowledge), skill review (find prompt improvements from chat history), experience (incorporate personal feedback), historical review (surface missed learnings from past sessions), and foundation (interview the user about unstated goals).

Ask the Board clones named public figures (Alex Hormozi, Andrew Huberman, and others with large public profiles) as agents inside the project, so a single slash-command routes a question through each expert perspective simultaneously; the buildpartner.ai plugin ships a preloaded version of this board.

Internal Focus Group, inspired by Amol from Anthropic cloning his manager Ami Vora for pre-submission feedback, clones specific named real people rather than archetypes, each with its own agent context and stated biases, so output is stress-tested against the actual target audience before it goes live.
